title: implement init containers in podman
body:
this is the first pass at implementing init containers for podman pods.
init containersare made popular by k8s as a way to run setup for pods
before the pods standard containers run.

unlike k8s, we support two styles of init containers: always and
oneshot.  always means the container stays in the pod and starts
whenever a pod is started.  this does not apply to pods restarting.
oneshot means the container runs onetime when the pod starts and then is
removed.

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body: i dont have the bandwidth to run the e2e tests so pushing here to test.

Having less containers than max capacity of the array is fine

On Sat, Jul 24, 2021 at 14:49 Brent Baude ***@***.***> wrote:

> ***@***.**** commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In libpod/pod_api.go
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11011#discussion_r676032329>:
>
> > @@ -449,12 +479,18 @@ func (p *Pod) Status() (map[string]define.ContainerStatus, error) {
>  	if !p.valid {
>  		return nil, define.ErrPodRemoved
>  	}
> -
> +	noInitCtrs := make([]*Container, 0)
>
> Should do make([]*Container, 0, len(allCtrs)) (and do this a few lines
> down so allCtrs exists)
>
> I don't believe this can be done because not allCtrs go into InitCtrs.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
>

> .

I test it on my machine, it works ok.

one drive-by nit, otherwise LGTM.

something to explore may be extending the pod state (status?) to support an initializing state (while init containers are running) so a `pod ps` shows it's initializing. `kubectl` does so by writing `Init:0/2` if the two init containers are initializing.

@baude @mheon @zhangguanzhang @rhatdan @haircommander  Just a suggestion not sure if it makes sense,  how about renaming ****--init-ctr**=*type*  **`oneshot`** to **`once`** i have seen **once** being used very often in **init-container extensions** for example: https://github.com/hashicorp/consul-template/blob/master/docs/modes.md#once-mode but i like `oneshot` also. :smiley: .

> @baude @mheon @zhangguanzhang @rhatdan @haircommander Just a suggestion not sure if it makes sense, how about renaming ****--init-ctr**=_type_ **`oneshot`** to **`once`** i have seen **once** being used very often in **init-container extensions** for example: https://github.com/hashicorp/consul-template/blob/master/docs/modes.md#once-mode but i like `oneshot` also. smiley .

I'd agree, **once** is a more common term.
